TRANSFORMING RURAL HEALTH ACROSS KANSAS

Transparency. Accountability. Better Health.

Kansas received $221.9 million in federal funding through the Rural Health Transformation Program to strengthen rural healthcare systems and improve patient outcomes across 105 counties.

Clinically Integrated Networks: Kansas High Value Network & PrairieLINK

Two non-overlapping rural hospital networks are emerging under the Rural Health Transformation Program, using different funding and affiliation models.

RHTP awards by network: two funding strategies

The two networks illustrate different approaches to network development and capital flow.

Kansas High Value Network

Independent cooperative
Members retain independent ownership and governance.
Distributed capital
Multiple hospitals receive direct RPGP or REH/CAP awards.
Network value
Shared services, scale, contracting, and referral coordination.
vs

PrairieLINK

Anchor-led model
Stormont Vail acts as the tertiary and administrative anchor.
Centralized capital
A larger share of funding flows through the anchor and selected campuses.
System value
Clinical integration, specialty access, workforce, and common infrastructure.

Kansas Hospital Intelligence Explorer

Search Kansas hospitals and examine CMS cost-report financial and operational performance, payer revenue, uncompensated care, quality, workforce, and technology. Financial and operating metrics are sourced from CMS Hospital Cost Reports (HCRIS).
